executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 106 Canadian organizations breached between June 8-18, 2026. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (consumer brand), National Money Mart (financial services with database leaked), Eastern Townships School Board (education), Municipality of North Perth, Peterborough Public Health, and Kids & Company (childcare). The Qilin ransomware group leads with 21 victims, followed by Play (11), Safepay (11), and Clop (13). Critical infrastructure sectors hit include healthcare, education, public sector, and financial services. No CVEs or zero-day vulnerabilities were reported in the intelligence feed.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 21 Canadian organizations including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, Traffic Tech, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Eastern Townships School Board, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), BioPharma Services, and multiple SMBs across transportation, technology, manufacturing, construction, and financial services sectors
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - data exfiltration and encryption for double extortion ransomware attacks

items.name: Play
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products, Makivik, ALLMAX, Security ONE Alarm Systems, Katch Kan, Turkstra Trusses, and Select Tool spanning agriculture, technology, manufacturing, and business services
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter) - known for rapid encryption and data theft operations

items.name: Safepay
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including briwaycarriers.com, mattandsteve.com (agriculture), ripobec.com, groupepiche.ca (construction), precisionaluminum.ca, kenalex.ca, silverlinegroupinc.com, mactavishco.ca, and teccart.qc.ca (education) across transportation, food production, construction, and manufacturing
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol) - double extortion ransomware operations

items.name: Clop
items.targets: 13 Canadian organizations including STRATEGICOBJECTIVES.COM, IDEALWELDERS.COM, CMHHELI.COM (helicopter transport), SMTLAWYERS.CA (legal), CLOUDDATAWORKS.CA, SENSIBLESOLUTIONS.CA (technology), WHISKIJACKRESORTS.COM, EASTPLATS.COM (energy), and multiple technology and business services firms
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567.002 (Exfiltration to Cloud Storage) - known for exploiting vulnerabilities in file transfer applications and mass data exfiltration

items.name: TheGentlemen
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Groupe Caddac (construction), ControlGMC (manufacturing), Labtician Ophthalmics (healthcare), Real Tech (manufacturing), and Colliers spanning construction, manufacturing, and healthcare sectors
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059.001 (PowerShell) - ransomware with data exfiltration capabilities

items.name: Dragonforce
items.targets: 6 Canadian organizations including phoenixlabs.com, CCR Solutions (technology), Esposito Bros. Construction, soteck.com (agriculture), McKee-Pownall Equine Services (veterinary), and Emond Publishing (education) across technology, construction, agriculture, healthcare, and education
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1489 (Service Stop) - ransomware operations with service disruption

items.name: Lynx
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Peterborough Public Health (public sector), laurysenkitchens.com (manufacturing), groupecho.com (business services), www.ktlgroup.com (technology), and Trucash (financial services) impacting public health, manufacturing, and financial sectors
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1005 (Data from Local System) - ransomware with focus on public sector and financial services

items.name: Incransom
items.targets: 8 Canadian organizations including CPK Interior, excavationtourigny.ca (construction), glasserstv.com, steelworksinc.ca (manufacturing), bisonfamilymedical.com (healthcare), omegatoolcorp.com, reidhurstnagy.com (financial), and baisyaakov.ca (education) across construction, manufacturing, healthcare, and education
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1083 (File and Directory Discovery) - ransomware targeting SMBs

items.name: AiLock
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Solutions Extreme Technology (technology) and Eeyou Communications Network (telecommunications) impacting technology and critical telecommunications infrastructure
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery) - ransomware targeting technology and telecom sectors

items.name: Sinobi
items.targets: 3 Canadian organizations including Active Green + Ross (automotive services), Morison Insurance Brokers (financial services), and Kids & Company (childcare services) impacting consumer services and financial sectors
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1003 (OS Credential Dumping) - ransomware with credential theft capabilities

items.name: Akira
items.targets: Inland Audio Visual (business services) in Canada
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1021.001 (Remote Desktop Protocol) - ransomware known for exploiting RDP and VPN vulnerabilities

items.name: Nightspire
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Regal Building Materials Ltd. (construction) and one redacted entity (HO** RIL** LLP)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - ransomware operations

items.name: Bravox
items.targets: John O's Foods (agriculture and food production) in Canada
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - ransomware targeting food production sector

items.name: Nitrogen
items.targets: Whitfield Welding Inc (manufacturing) in Canada
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - ransomware targeting manufacturing

items.name: Everest
items.targets: National Money Mart Company (financial services) in Canada with database leaked - major data breach
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1005 (Data from Local System) - double extortion with confirmed database exfiltration and leak

items.name: Securotrop
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Living Realty (business services) and Cadman Power Equipment (manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - ransomware operations

items.name: Medusa
items.targets: MRC de Maskinongé district (public sector municipality) in Quebec, Canada
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - ransomware targeting municipal government

items.name: Devman
items.targets: 4 Canadian organizations including beausejourco-op.crs (agriculture), Axion50plus (financial services), ravand.com (technology), and one redacted entity
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - ransomware targeting agriculture and financial services

items.name: Anubis
items.targets: One law firm in Canada (business services) - identity redacted
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - ransomware targeting legal sector with high-value data

items.name: Worldleaks
items.targets: Municipality of North Perth, Canada (public sector) - municipal government
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - ransomware targeting municipal infrastructure

items.name: CoinbaseCartel
items.targets: Canada Goose (consumer services) - major Canadian retail brand
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - ransomware targeting high-profile consumer brand

items.name: Payload
items.targets: Q2 Artificial Lift Services (manufacturing) in Canada
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - ransomware targeting manufacturing sector

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is experiencing a severe ransomware crisis with 106 organizations breached on June 8, 2026 alone, spanning all critical infrastructure sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (major retail brand), National Money Mart (financial services with confirmed database leak), Peterborough Public Health, Municipality of North Perth, Eastern Townships School Board, and Kids & Company childcare chain. No CCCS advisories were published in the reporting period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (consumer services brand) breached by CoinbaseCartel ransomware; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) breached by Sinobi ransomware
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company breached by Everest ransomware with database leaked; Morison Insurance Brokers breached by Sinobi; Trucash financial services breached by Lynx; multiple accounting and financial services firms impacted including Axion50plus (Devman), reidhurstnagy.com (Incransom), Pewarchuk CPA (Play), and MG Chartered Professional Accountant (Qilin)

report_2w.executive_summary: Cisco is warning of an unpatched zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-20245) in SD-WAN products actively exploited in the wild—this is the seventh SD-WAN zero-day exploited in 2026. Attackers can escalate privileges to root with no patch currently available. Separately, a critical flaw in Everest Forms Pro WordPress plugin is being exploited to take over websites. The Canadian Cyber Centre (CCCS) issued an alert regarding a security incident impacting GitHub's internal repositories, though details remain limited. Multiple ransomware groups (Akira, Qilin, DragonForce) continue aggressive extortion campaigns, and npm supply chain attacks including IronWorm malware hit 36 packages this week.

items.description: Cisco SD-WAN zero-day allowing arbitrary command execution and root escalation

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-013 on May 29 regarding a security incident affecting GitHub's internal repositories, though specific impacts on Canadian organizations remain unclear. No major Canadian retail, financial, or telecom incidents were identified in this reporting period. The Ontario Home Builders' Association was reported as a victim of the Qilin ransomware group.
